title: Feed v0.2.2
content:
Feed and the JeffersonWM public shell both got a quieter presentation pass in this checkpoint. The goal was not to add new systems, but to make the public-facing surfaces feel more intentional, easier to reset, and more visually consistent with the newer background work happening elsewhere.

What changed:

  • Feed title now resets the page: clicking Feed at the top returns the interface to its default view, clears active filters, closes open editor/login panels, jumps back to the newest week, and scrolls to the top.
  • Shared patterned backgrounds added: Feed and JeffersonWM Home now use the new lion pattern at a subtle 5% opacity, stored in stable per-app background folders so the visual layer survives future builds cleanly.
  • Battalion public polish continued: Battalion's landing, login/register, and public dashboard were updated to use the new illustrated background treatment, with softer alternating feed-row grays so the public page reads as one continuous surface.

This is a presentation-and-operations checkpoint: the public pages are a little calmer, the reset behavior is more practical, and the asset organization is cleaner for future background experiments.
